steps.title: Find the source of doubt
steps.content: Ecologists express reservations about traditional descriptions after comparing them with recent observations. The blank must name a mismatch that would make those descriptions seem less useful.

steps.title: Remove nonmismatch words
steps.content:
• Choice A means declarations and does not explain doubt.
  • Choice B means similarities, which would support the descriptions.
  • Choice C means repeated appearances, not conflict between descriptions and observations.

steps.title: Name the disagreement
steps.content: Discrepancies between the traditional descriptions and recent observations would naturally lead ecologists to question the descriptions' utility. The answer is D.
